formulas.name: Content
formulas.formula:
```json
[
  {
    "name": "Adam Smith's Concept of Division of Labour",
    "formula": "Specialization leads to increase in productivity",
    "variables": "Specialization",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "This concept leads to efficiency and growth in the economy."
  },
  {
    "name": "Law of Diminishing Marginal Utility",
    "formula": "As the quantity of a commodity increases, the marginal utility decreases",
    "variables": "Quantity of the commodity, Marginal Utility",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "This concept helps in understanding consumer behavior."
  },
  {
    "name": "Concept of Opportunity Cost",
    "formula": "Opportunity cost is the value of the next best alternative given up",
    "variables": "Opportunity Cost, Next Best Alternative",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "This concept helps in understanding the trade-offs involved in decision-making."
  },
  {
    "name": "Gains from Trade",
    "formula": "Trade between two countries leads to gains from specialization and trade",
    "variables": "Specialization, Trade",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "This concept helps in understanding the concept of comparative advantage."
  },
  {
    "name": "Concept of Comparative Advantage",
    "formula": "A country should specialize in producing goods in which it has a comparative advantage",
    "variables": "Comparative Advantage, Specialization",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "This concept helps in understanding the benefits of trade."
  },
  {
    "name": "Concept of Absolute Advantage",
    "formula": "A country has an absolute advantage if it can produce a good at a lower opportunity cost",
    "variables": "Absolute Advantage, Opportunity Cost",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "This concept helps in understanding the concept of specialization."
  },
  {
    "name": "Gains from Globalization",
    "formula": "Globalization leads to gains from trade and specialization",
    "variables": "Trade, Specialization, Globalization",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "This concept helps in understanding the benefits of globalization."
  },
  {
    "name": "Liberalization",
    "formula": "Removal of restrictions on entry and exit of firms in an industry",
    "variables": "Liberalization, Restrictions, Firms",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "This concept helps in understanding the reforms in the economy."
  },
  {
    "name": "Privatization",
    "formula": "Transfer of ownership and control of a public enterprise to the private sector",
    "variables": "Privatization, Public Enterprise, Private Sector",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "This concept helps in understanding the reforms in the economy."
  },
  {
    "name": "Globalization",
    "formula": "Increased integration of national economies with the global economy",
    "variables": "Globalization, National Economies, Global Economy",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "This concept helps in understanding the interdependence of economies."
  },
  {
    "name": "Adam Smith's Concept of Invisible Hand",
    "formula": "Invisible hand leads to efficient allocation of resources",
    "variables": "Invisible Hand, Efficient Allocation of Resources",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "This concept helps in understanding the self-regulating nature of market economies."
  },
  {
    "name": "Concept of Economic Incentives",
    "formula": "Economic incentives lead to efficient allocation of resources",
    "variables": "Economic Incentives, Efficient Allocation of Resources",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "This concept helps in understanding the role of incentives in decision-making."
  }
]
```

Note: There are no specific mathematical formulas in the chapter Liberalisation, Privatisation and Globalisation of Class 11 Economics. The above JSON array includes key statements and laws that are relevant to the chapter.
